jurisdictions.name: United Kingdom
jurisdictions.instrument: Online Safety Act 2023 + Ofcom 'Highly Effective Age Assurance' (HEAA) codes
jurisdictions.mandate: Pornography providers and platforms serving content harmful to children must deploy highly effective age assurance (technically accurate, robust, reliable, fair). Self-declaration insufficient.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Facial age estimation (AI); photo-ID + facial recognition/liveness; open banking; mobile-network-operator checks; credit card; reusable digital-ID wallets; email-based estimation
jurisdictions.status: In force and enforced. ~76 sites under investigation; fines exceeding GBP 1m issued.
jurisdictions.notes: Method-agnostic but pushes platforms toward AI facial age estimation as lowest-friction compliant option.

jurisdictions.name: United Kingdom
jurisdictions.instrument: BritCard national digital ID (Starmer government policy)
jurisdictions.mandate: Announced national digital ID; initially proposed mandatory to prove right to work; compulsion walked back January 2026. Full scheme targeted 2029.
jurisdictions.status: Policy stage, not yet primary legislation. Highly contested (petition ~2.9m signatures). First credential (Veteran Card) live 17 Oct 2025.

jurisdictions.name: Australia
jurisdictions.instrument: Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act 2024
jurisdictions.mandate: Age-restricted social media platforms must take 'reasonable steps' to prevent under-16s from holding accounts (Facebook, Instagram, Threads, TikTok, Snapchat, Reddit, X, YouTube, Twitch, Kick). Penalties up to ~A$49.5m.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Age assurance not prescribed; in practice age estimation/inference, ID checks, third-party verification. eSafety discourages sole reliance on government ID.
jurisdictions.status: In force. ~4.7m under-16 accounts removed by mid-Dec 2025. eSafety + OAIC co-regulate.
jurisdictions.notes: World-first national under-16 social media ban.

jurisdictions.name: Australia
jurisdictions.mandate: Government-commissioned evidence base, not a mandate. Tested 48 vendors / 60+ technologies.
jurisdictions.tech_required: AI facial age estimation; ID verification; parental controls
jurisdictions.status: Published. Found age assurance viable but no universal solution; facial age estimation error commonly ~18 months, worse for girls, First Nations, lower-SES groups.
jurisdictions.notes: Key public benchmark showing biometric age-estimation accuracy limits.

jurisdictions.name: Australia
jurisdictions.mandate: Voluntary accreditation scheme for digital-ID providers; identity-proofing levels, privacy, security standards. Government system (AGDIS) built on myID.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Accredited digital identity with proofing levels; biometric face-matching at higher assurance levels
jurisdictions.status: Live. >15m myIDs, ~80m verified transactions by Dec 2025. Private-sector expansion targeted Dec 2026. ACCC = regulator.
jurisdictions.notes: Provides identity rails that age-assurance schemes can leverage.

jurisdictions.name: European Union
jurisdictions.mandate: Each Member State must offer an EUDI Wallet to all citizens; large platforms must accept it where strong identification is required.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Interoperable digital identity wallet; selective disclosure; age attestation
jurisdictions.status: Toolbox/Architecture Reference Framework being finalised; national rollouts ramping into 2026.
jurisdictions.notes: Foundational identity layer underpinning EU age verification.

jurisdictions.name: European Union
jurisdictions.mandate: Provides an age-verification app ahead of full EUDI wallets. Pilots: France, Denmark, Greece, Italy, Spain, Cyprus, Ireland.
jurisdictions.tech_required: On-device zero-knowledge proofs (cryptographic yes/no); eID/passport/ID-card onboarding. No identifying data leaves device.
jurisdictions.status: Pilot phase. v2 blueprint 10 Oct 2025; technical readiness ~15 Apr 2026; app-store distribution summer 2026.
jurisdictions.notes: Privacy-by-design alternative to biometric estimation; ZKP rather than raw face data.

jurisdictions.name: European Union
jurisdictions.instrument: DSA Article 28 — Guidelines on Protection of Minors
jurisdictions.mandate: Platforms accessible to minors must take appropriate/proportionate measures incl. age assurance. Methods must be accurate, reliable, robust, non-intrusive, non-discriminatory. Voluntary but used as compliance benchmark.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Age assurance up to verification; ZKP-based mini-wallet recommended
jurisdictions.status: Guidance live; Commission using DSA enforcement to push age verification.
jurisdictions.notes: Soft-law benchmark backed by hard DSA enforcement powers.

jurisdictions.name: European Union
jurisdictions.mandate: Softened text drops mandatory message scanning (now voluntary). Providers must take 'all reasonable mitigation measures' including age verification and age assessment.
jurisdictions.status: Not final. Trilogue ongoing; interim voluntary-scanning derogation expires 2026; outcome uncertain.
jurisdictions.notes: Encryption-breaking mandate defeated; age verification survives as core obligation.

jurisdictions.name: United States (Federal - SCOTUS)
jurisdictions.instrument: Free Speech Coalition v. Paxton (606 U.S. ___)
jurisdictions.mandate: Upheld 6-3 Texas HB 1181 age-verification law for sites with >1/3 content harmful to minors; intermediate scrutiny. Removes principal First Amendment obstacle to state porn age-verification laws.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Greenlights government-ID upload / digital-ID / age-estimation age checks
jurisdictions.status: Binding precedent; >20 states have such laws.
jurisdictions.notes: Pivotal enabler of the US state age-verification wave.

jurisdictions.name: United States (States - porn)
jurisdictions.instrument: Louisiana Act 440 (HB 142) + 20+ state successors (TX HB 1181, UT, AR, MS, AZ, SC, VA, etc.)
jurisdictions.mandate: Sites with >=1/3 material harmful to minors must verify users' age. LA penalties up to $5,000/day plus $10,000/violation.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Government-ID verification (LA Wallet digital ID / driver's license); commercial vendors (ID upload, facial age estimation, transactional data)
jurisdictions.status: Broadly in force post-Paxton; some platforms geo-block non-compliant states.
jurisdictions.notes: Louisiana model (LA Wallet) links porn age-gating directly to state digital ID.

jurisdictions.name: United States (States - app stores)
jurisdictions.instrument: App Store Accountability Acts (Utah SB 142, Texas SB 2420, LA, AL, CA Digital Age Assurance Act)
jurisdictions.effective_date: Utah 2025-05-07 (comply by 2026-05-06); Texas in effect ~2026-06 under 5th Cir. stay
jurisdictions.mandate: App stores must verify user age at account creation and obtain parental consent for minors before downloads/in-app purchases; share age/consent signals with developers.
jurisdictions.tech_required: OS/app-store-layer age verification (age-category determination, ID or inference) + parental-consent flows
jurisdictions.status: Utah in effect (ramp to May 2026); Texas in effect under 5th Cir. stay (28 May 2026) pending merits; ongoing First Amendment litigation.
jurisdictions.notes: Shifts age-verification burden to Apple/Google at the device layer.

jurisdictions.name: United States (Federal - KOSA)
jurisdictions.instrument: Kids Online Safety Act (S.1748 / H.R.6484; House KIDS Act)
jurisdictions.mandate: If enacted: duty of care + safeguards for 'known' minors (House/Senate differ on knowledge standard). Pushes age-knowledge inference, not explicit age verification.
jurisdictions.status: Not enacted. Reintroduced 14 May 2025; House KIDS Act advanced out of subcommittee Mar 2026; versions diverge; uncertain.
jurisdictions.notes: Federal counterpart still stalled; states are the active front.

jurisdictions.name: United States (States - mDL/mobile ID)
jurisdictions.instrument: State mDL programs; ISO/IEC 18013-5; AAMVA guidelines; DHS/TSA acceptance
jurisdictions.effective_date: ISO 18013-5 published 2021; ~21 states + PR TSA-accepted by Jan 2026
jurisdictions.mandate: Voluntary mobile credential; increasingly the rails for age verification and identity.
jurisdictions.tech_required: ISO 18013-5 cryptographically signed mobile credential (Apple/Google/Samsung Wallet); enrollment often uses facial matching/liveness
jurisdictions.status: Expanding. CA ~2.65m mDLs (Aug 2025); IL Apple Wallet IDs Nov 2025 with selfie + liveness.
jurisdictions.notes: Verifier ecosystem and privacy norms still maturing; biometrics at enrollment.

jurisdictions.name: China
jurisdictions.instrument: Measures for Administration of National Network Identity Authentication Public Services (CyberID / wanghao-wangzheng)
jurisdictions.mandate: State-operated centralized real-name authentication. Issues network number + network credential so users authenticate without giving real names/ID numbers to each platform. Officially voluntary.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Government-issued cryptographic ID token; app-based auth; underpinned by real-name + facial-recognition infrastructure
jurisdictions.notes: Centralizes the real-name layer under MPS/CAC; surveillance concerns.

jurisdictions.name: China
jurisdictions.instrument: Real-name registration + Regulations on Protection of Minors in Cyberspace + CAC 'Minor Mode'
jurisdictions.effective_date: Minor Mode launched 2025-04-29; gaming limits since 2021
jurisdictions.mandate: Minors' online gaming limited to 1 hour, 8-9pm, weekends/holidays only; device/app/app-store 'minor mode' with daily limits, 10pm-6am blackout, break reminders, content filters; real-name registration across platforms.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Real-name verification (ID + facial recognition for gaming login spot-checks)
jurisdictions.status: In force; enforcement uneven (~77% of minors evade via relatives' identities).
jurisdictions.notes: World's most prescriptive minor-protection regime; uses facial recognition.

jurisdictions.name: Russia
jurisdictions.instrument: Presidential decree (Jun 2025) on state messenger MAX; SIM-to-Gosuslugi binding; VPN restrictions
jurisdictions.mandate: Every SIM must be tied to a Gosuslugi (state services) account; unregistered SIMs deactivated. MAX messenger mandatory pre-install; Gosuslugi mobile access funneled through MAX. State digital ID piloted via MAX.
jurisdictions.tech_required: State identity binding (Gosuslugi); government messenger as identity/auth chokepoint; deep-packet inspection (TSPU)
jurisdictions.status: Active and tightening. Roskomnadzor blocked 469 VPN services (Feb 2026); Telegram/WhatsApp throttled.
jurisdictions.notes: Sovereign-internet identity layer; control-oriented rather than child-protection.

jurisdictions.name: Iran
jurisdictions.instrument: National Information Network (NIN); Supreme Council of Cyberspace 'white SIM' system; VPN-criminalization bill
jurisdictions.effective_date: NIN since ~2013; white-SIM system formally instituted 2025-07
jurisdictions.mandate: SIM cards/devices linked to national identity records; two-tier internet (government-approved 'white SIM' unrestricted access vs. filtered public). VPN production/use criminalized.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Identity-bound SIM registration; NIN sovereign network layer; whitelisting; VPN detection/neutralization
jurisdictions.status: Active, intensified during 2025-2026 protests (near-total blackout Jan 2026, NIN-only whitelisted access).
jurisdictions.notes: Identity controls serve censorship/surveillance, not age assurance.

jurisdictions.name: India
jurisdictions.instrument: Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 + DPDP Rules 2025 (Rule 10); Aadhaar
jurisdictions.mandate: Under-18 = child. Verifiable parental consent required before processing children's data; platforms must confirm parent is adult, child is minor, and genuine relationship. Behavioral profiling/targeted ads to children prohibited. Penalties up to INR 200 crore.
jurisdictions.tech_required: Identity/age verification; Aadhaar-linked DigiLocker tokens designated authoritative credential; virtual tokens from authorized entities
jurisdictions.status: In force; compliance ramp ongoing; parental-consent mechanics debated as impractical.
jurisdictions.notes: Builds on Aadhaar (~1.4bn enrolled); DigiLocker as the verification rail.
